## Reviewer's Guide This PR resolves three critical V12 DNA violations by restoring zero-allocation signal structs, tightening exception handling semantics (distinguishing known NT8 quirks from unexpected failures), and adding positionÔÇæsafety fallbacks in flatten/stop-sync paths, while also bringing events and handlers back in line with .NET conventions and addressing multiple static analysis issues. #### Sequence diagram for emergency flatten fallback in FlattenAllApexAccounts ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Strategy participant SIMA_Flatten as SIMA_Flatten participant NT8 as NinjaTraderEngine Strategy->>SIMA_Flatten: FlattenAllApexAccounts() loop Dequeue pending ops SIMA_Flatten->>NT8: TriggerCustomEvent(PumpFlattenOps, null) alt TriggerCustomEvent throws InvalidOperationException SIMA_Flatten-->>Strategy: Print("[FLATTEN] WARNING: TriggerCustomEvent failed") else TriggerCustomEvent throws Exception SIMA_Flatten->>SIMA_Flatten: _pendingFlattenOps.TryDequeue() loop For each FlattenWorkItem SIMA_Flatten->>SIMA_Flatten: ProcessFlattenWorkItem_CancelOrders(workItem, acct) alt !workItem.CancelOnly SIMA_Flatten->>SIMA_Flatten: ProcessFlattenWorkItem_ClosePositions(workItem, acct) end SIMA_Flatten->>SIMA_Flatten: SetExpectedPositionLocked(ExpKey(acct.Name), 0) end SIMA_Flatten-->>Strategy: Print("[FLATTEN] CRITICAL: Unexpected error...") end end ``` #### Sequence diagram for stop sync emergency flatten in CreateNewStopOrder ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Strategy participant StopSync as StopSyncManager participant NT8 as NinjaTraderEngine Strategy->>StopSync: UpdateStopQuantity(entryName, pos) alt Need new stop StopSync->>StopSync: CreateNewStopOrder(entryName, qty, price, direction) StopSync->>NT8: SubmitOrderUnmanaged(...) alt InvalidOperationException (SubmitOrderUnmanaged / CreateOrder) StopSync-->>Strategy: Print("WARNING CreateNewStopOrder (known quirk)") else Exception StopSync-->>Strategy: Print("CRITICAL CreateNewStopOrder...") StopSync->>StopSync: FlattenPositionByName(entryName) alt Emergency flatten throws StopSync-->>Strategy: Print("CRITICAL: Emergency flatten also failed") end end end ``` ### File-Level Changes | Change | Details | Files | | ------ | ------- | ----- | | Harden flatten/stop-sync and order-management paths with position-safe fallback behavior and non-fail-fast handling of known NT8 quirks. |
  • In SIMA.Flatten and Orders.Management.Flatten, add granular catch filters for known InvalidOperationException patterns (TriggerCustomEvent/order ops/MMIO/collection modified) that log warnings, avoid rethrows, and ensure guards/queues are released so remaining accounts can continue flattening.
  • Implement emergency fallback flatten logic that drains pending flatten queues and executes synchronous flatten per account with detailed logging, while suppressing rethrows to prioritize position safety.
  • In Orders.Management.StopSync, wrap stop quantity updates and new stop creation in filtered catches for known NT8 issues, add emergency flatten on critical stop creation failures, and explicitly avoid rethrowing to keep positions protected.
  • Adjust various callbacks (OnExecutionUpdate, OnOrderUpdate, ReconcileOrphanedOrders, REAPER marshal-flatten, CIT chaser) to distinguish between known fragile paths (suppressed via filters) and unexpected exceptions (logged as CRITICAL, sometimes rethrown).

## Walkthrough This PR systematically standardizes error handling across the trading strategy codebase and updates the signal broadcasting model to .NET conventions. Event broadcasts transition from `Action` to `EventHandler` delegates. Exception handling is refined to distinguish known NinjaTrader quirks (logged as warnings, continue) from unexpected failures (logged as critical, rethrow immediately). Changes affect entry execution, order callbacks, dispatch operations, UI interactions, and IPC communication. ## Changes **Exception Handling and Signal Broadcasting Standardization** |Layer / File(s)|Summary| |---|---| |**Signal Broadcaster Event Model Update**
